mainEntity.name: How much does a Jaguar F-Type P450 engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned Jaguar F-Type P450 engine (AJ133 5.0 SC) typically costs £6,500–£10,000 supply only. Fitting adds £1,500–£2,500. These are specialist-only replacements — the supercharged V8 requires Jaguar V8 expertise. A timing chain job alone costs £2,000–£4,500.

mainEntity.name: Is the Jaguar F-Type P450 engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: The AJ133 5.0L V8 is fundamentally robust, but early examples (pre-2015) are known for timing chain guide wear — the defining issue of the engine family. The P450's 450PS tune is less stressed than the SVR's 575PS, but still puts significant heat into the cooling system. The single most important factor is oil change frequency — 5,000–7,500 mile intervals are essential. JLR revised the timing chain guides in 2015 with steel inserts.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the Jaguar F-Type P450?

acceptedAnswer.text: Timing chain guide wear (pre-2015 design, cold-start rattle, £2,000–£4,500), plastic coolant crossover pipe failure (coolant loss, overheating, £800–£2,000), and supercharger coupler wear (rattling at idle, £500–£1,000). Cooling system components are a particular concern on the 450PS tune.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a Jaguar F-Type P450 engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: F-Type P450 values range from £40,000–£80,000 depending on condition and mileage. A £6,500–£10,000 reconditioned engine represents 10–20% of vehicle value — well within our 60% Jaguar scrap threshold. For almost any example, replacement is financially justified.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my Jaguar F-Type P450 engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: The P450 uses the AJ133 engine code — Jaguar Land Rover's 5.0-litre supercharged V8, tuned to 450PS for this application. The code is stamped on the engine block; your VIN and vehicle documentation will also confirm the specification.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair the timing chain issue, or does it need replacement?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — if caught early. Timing chain and guide replacement costs £2,000–£4,500 and is significantly cheaper than a full engine replacement (£6,500–£10,000). JLR revised the guide design in 2015 with steel inserts. At the first sign of cold-start rattle, book an inspection immediately — ignored chain wear can lead to catastrophic piston-valve contact.

replacementCosts.h2: Jaguar F-Type P450 Engine Replacement Costs
replacementCosts.figuresNote: Figures above reflect the AJ133 5.0 SC engine in P450 specification. The 450PS tune is lower than the SVR's 575PS, making this a relatively accessible supercharged V8. All pricing is indicative; actual costs vary by mileage, condition and workshop location.
replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting an F-Type P450 engine typically takes 14–18 hours at £80–£120/hr (independent Jaguar specialist) or £140–£190/hr (London/SE dealer). The supercharger and cooling system add complexity.

repairBuyOrReplace.h2: Jaguar F-Type P450 — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: Preventative replacement before failure — always cheaper than engine replacement
Fit aftermarket metal pipes for a permanent fix
Replace as a set — common on high-mileage AJ133s
Replacement often £6,500–£10,000 — assess vehicle value carefully

buyingChecks.title: Buying This Jaguar F-Type P450? Check These First
buyIf: * Documented timing chain guide history (post-2015 build or retrofit)
* Coolant crossover pipes replaced with metal aftermarket units
* Full service history with regular oil changes (5,000–7,500 miles)
avoidIf: * Any evidence of coolant loss or overheating (crossover pipe failure risk)
* Cold-start rattle without documented timing chain work
* Poor service history — the AJ133 is unforgiving of extended oil change intervals
* Engine warning light with no diagnosis — these engines can hide expensive problems

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The F-Type P450 is the purist's F-Type — a 450PS supercharged V8 in a stunning body. The AJ133 engine is fundamentally robust and characterful, but timing chain guides and cooling system components demand vigilance. A post-2015 car with documented timing chain history and metal coolant pipes is the gold standard. Buy with your eyes open, maintain religiously, and the P450 will reward you.

cta.label: → Compare Jaguar F-Type P450 engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used Jaguar F-Type P450 (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Listen for any rattle on startup (timing chain guide wear warning)
* Service History — Oil change intervals are critical — 5,000–7,500 miles is the safe zone
* Coolant Level — Check for any loss; inspect around crossover pipes for leaks
* Supercharger Health — Any rattling from the supercharger at idle? (coupler wear)
* Engine Performance — Test acceleration; any hesitation, flat spots, or rough idle?
* Oil Consumption — Monitor between services; AJ133s can consume oil in hard use
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Any unresolved issues?
* MOT History — Check for emissions-related failures
